steps.title: Identify the function of "quindoxin (C₈H₆N₂O₂)"
steps.content: "Quindoxin" is the specific name of the compound — it's essential to identifying which compound we're discussing. Without it, "the gaseous compound" would be incomplete and vague.

steps.title: Apply the restrictive appositive rule
steps.content:
Rule: a restrictive appositive that identifies which specific thing is meant takes no commas. "Quindoxin" tells us which gaseous compound — without it, the sentence says only "the gaseous compound," leaving the referent vague. The name is essential.

A: "...gaseous compound, quindoxin (C8H6N2O2), has..." — commas on both sides signal a nonrestrictive appositive (optional extra info). The name is not optional here. Incorrect.

B: "...gaseous compound quindoxin (C8H6N2O2) has..." — no commas; quindoxin directly and necessarily identifies the compound. Correct.

C: "...gaseous compound quindoxin (C8H6N2O2), has..." — trailing comma only; unmatched and incorrectly places a boundary before the verb. Incorrect.

D: "...gaseous compound, quindoxin (C8H6N2O2) has..." — opening comma only; unmatched and incorrectly signals a nonrestrictive appositive without a closing comma. Incorrect.
